poster_title: Tree Search for LLM Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yuxiang Ji, Ziyu Ma, Yong Wang, Guanhua Chen, Xiangxiang Chu, Liaoni Wu

content: Recent advances in RL have improved LLMs' agentic capabilities.
LLM-based agents show gains in single-turn tasks with outcome rewards.
Multi-turn agent settings are crucial for next-gen foundation models.
However, heavy budget and sparse supervision pose challenges.
Tree-GRPO aims to address these issues efficiently.
High computational cost and time consumption in LLM rollouts.
Difficulty in identifying specific actions contributing to outcomes.
Sparse supervision in long-horizon, multi-turn trajectories.
Tree-GRPO uses an online rollout strategy based on tree search.
Tree structure yields interleaved trajectories with shared prefixes.
Estimates relative advantages at intra-tree and inter-tree levels.
panels.figure_caption: Overview of the Tree-GRPO training pipeline with tree-search rollout.

content: Evaluated on 11 datasets: single-hop, multi-hop, and web-agent tasks.
Compared with chain-based methods under limited rollout budget.
panels.figure_caption: Comparison of chain-based and tree-based sampling strategies in LLM multi-turn agent.

content: Achieved superior performance with only a quarter of the rollout budget.
Enabled a base model to adopt multi-turn agent interaction without fine-tuning.
Tree-GRPO effectively addresses heavy budget and sparse supervision challenges.
Consistent improvements and scalability make it a promising approach.
Future work can explore further optimizations and applications.

Fig1.caption: Figure 1: Comparison of chain-based and tree-based sampling strategies in LLM multi-turn agent
Fig1.description: 比较了链式和树形采样策略在多轮语言模型代理中的效果。

Fig2.caption: Figure 3: The overview of the Tree-GRPO training pipeline. The rollout is conducted in a tree-search
Fig2.description: 展示了 Tree-GRPO 训练管道的概览，其中 rollout 在树搜索中进行。
